body.p: The Content Pump for MarkLogic is a Java toolTools for MarkLogicContent Pump that enables bulk import of data via the command line.
The relevant GitHub project can be found here GitHub Project for the MarkLogic Content Pump.
For a simple installation, you can also download the binaries from the
Developer pages Binaries for installing the MarkLogic Content Pump.
I use the following Bash script to upload data to MarkLogic:
body.pre:
#!/bin/bash
set -eo pipefail
mlcp_opts="-database alex-test -host localhost -username admin -password admin"
mlcp import $mlcp_opts \ -input_file_path input-files \ -input_file_type aggregates \ -aggregate_record_element chapter \ -output_collections /chapter \ -output_uri_prefix /chapter/ \ -output_uri_suffix .xml
body.p: All files in the folder input-files are imported. The file type of the data to be uploaded is specified as aggregates. These are XML data.

note.p: More information about the command line options can be found on the corresponding documentation page Documentation page for the import options of the MarkLogic Content Pump from MarkLogic.

body.p: With the option -aggregate_record_element, it is defined that the input should be split with respect to the <chapter> element. I.e. a file with the following content:
body.pre: <test> <title>Test Datei</title> <chapter> <title>Test Kapitel 1</title> <content>Kapitel Inhalt 1</content> </chapter> <chapter> <title>Test Kapitel 2</title> <content>Kapitel Inhalt 2</content> </chapter> <chapter> <title>Test Kapitel 2</title> <content>Kapitel Inhalt 2</content> </chapter> </test>

fig.title: Result of a MarkLogic Content Pump Session
fig.desc: On the console, you can view the result of the [[code:mlcp]] session. As desired, three XML fragments were stored separately in the collection.

note.p: To avoid memory problems in MarkLogic, it is recommended to import large documents that you only want to "store" with the option -document_type binary. In this context, the option -streaming true is also of interest.

body.p: Another important point that I noticed when working with mlcp:

messagepanel.typeofhazard: If inconsistencies occur in the data storage, it may be because imports were made from the same file (same filename in the filesystem) in different [[code:mlcp]] sessions.
messagepanel.howtoavoid: Care must be taken to ensure that file names are unique. This can be achieved, for example, by assigning a unique ID in the file name. The documentation page for the [[code:mlcp]] options states the following:

howtoavoid.i: "If your aggregate URI id's are not unique, you can overwrite one document in your input set with another. Importing documents with non-unique URI id's from multiple threads can also cause deadlocks."

"The generated URIs are unique across a single import operation, but they are not globally unique. For example, if you repeatedly import data from some file /tmp/data.csv, the generated URIs will be the same each time (modulo differences in the number of documents inserted by the job)"
